meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategies for Freelancers: Advanced Guide
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Master SaaS pricing strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-value, scalable solutions that impress clients.
content.answer_block: To escape the 'basic app' trap, pivot from flat-fee development to value-based SaaS pricing models. By integrating dynamic billing architectures with automated subscription logic, you transform your deliverables into revenue-generating engines. Use Antigravity to scaffold the infrastructure and Claude Code to refine your Stripe API implementations for professional, production-ready results.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the client wants a 'simple' app, but the project scope creeps until you're burning midnight oil for a flat fee that barely covers your coffee. You’re trapped in the cycle of building commodity software, where every hour spent is an hour lost. It’s time to stop being a code monkey and start being a SaaS architect.

True professional leverage comes from moving beyond feature-based pricing. By architecting scalable, subscription-ready SaaS foundations, you shift the conversation from 'what does it cost' to 'what is the ROI.' This tutorial gives you the technical blueprint to build high-ticket, recurring-revenue applications using modern AI-assisted workflows.

steps.title: Architecting the Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Define your schema to handle multiple subscription levels. We focus on decoupling feature access from the user object to ensure future scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema and TypeScript interface for a SaaS application that supports three distinct pricing tiers (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) with a mapping system for feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Generate a Zod schema and Prisma model for a Subscription table linked to a User model.'

steps.title: Implementing Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Securely handle payment events to update user access levels in real-time, ensuring your app state always matches the payment provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust Next.js API route to handle Stripe webhook events, specifically focusing on customer.subscription.updated and invoice.payment_succeeded to update local database states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a webhook handler in src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts with proper signature verification.'

steps.title: Dynamic Feature Gating
steps.description: Implement a middleware-based system that checks subscription status before allowing access to premium routes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a reusable middleware utility that checks the user's subscription tier against requested route permissions and redirects unauthorized users to the pricing page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit 'Add a subscription guard middleware in src/middleware.ts that enforces tier-based access control.'

steps.title: Building the Pricing Table UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting UI component that highlights the 'Pro' tier and dynamically fetches pricing data from the backend.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React pricing component using Tailwind CSS that fetches plan data from a server-side function and handles the checkout redirection flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /generate 'Create a pricing card component with a toggle for monthly/yearly billing and integrated Stripe checkout links.'

steps.title: Automating Usage Limits
steps.description: Implement metered billing logic to prevent abuse and create a natural upsell path for high-usage clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a service function to increment usage counters in Redis and trigger a subscription upgrade prompt if the user exceeds their tier quota.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a usage-tracking service that integrates with Redis and checks quotas before executing protected actions.'

pro_tips: Always price in tiers of three: a decoy entry, a high-value middle, and an expensive enterprise anchor to drive conversions.
Use Redis for rate-limiting and usage tracking to keep your primary database lean and responsive.
Implement 'soft-gating'—show the premium UI but blur the content, which creates more desire than a hidden page.
Automate your invoice emails via Stripe to reduce support overhead; let the system handle the billing lifecycle.
Never hardcode pricing; always fetch plan configurations from your database so you can adjust prices without a redeploy.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing infrastructure to a staging environment, run a load test on your webhook handlers, and present the new dashboard to your client as a value-add feature.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for small projects and start architecting high-value SaaS ecosystems that generate recurring revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
